page: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical core with Hyper-Threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and is supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1845.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1802.0. However, multi-core and memory performance metrics are generally lower. The stress-ng multi-core CPU score is 1895.08, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 2177.0, both falling into the weak or poor categories. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 13.5 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 14.89 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the m7i.large is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale application hosting. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), which can assist with specific vector and matrix math operations. However, due to its limited physical core count and lower multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks, it is not recommended for intensive database operations, heavy multi-threaded compiling, or large-scale data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 3.2 GHz and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides dedicated CPU allocation but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1802.0, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and text processing, rather than memory-intensive or highly multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor, suitable for lightweight workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A general-purpose instance featuring DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for lightweight workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz
Features 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications and basic web serving
